call_meta.persona: Polish (EU) freelance illustrator, four years in the Netherlands, settling. Belonging + Career (winning Dutch clients) + Integration motivations. Barriers: Production gap (pronunciation), Prior course skepticism, budget. As an EU national she is outside the inburgering ICP, but she is a strong fit for the broader 'want to actually speak Dutch' market. No legal deadline; timing blocked until around October.
participants: prospect -- Polish freelance illustrator, 4 years in NL

jtbd.job: Reach functional Dutch for small talk with clients
jtbd.desired_outcome: Win more Dutch clients by being able to make small talk and do business in Dutch
jtbd.current_approach: Avoids speaking; understands a lot but can't produce it

jtbd.job: Integrate and meet non-expat Dutch people
jtbd.desired_outcome: Feel part of the community now that she has decided to settle; build connections beyond the expat circle
jtbd.current_approach: Spends time with Dutch people to absorb vocabulary informally

jtbd.desired_outcome: Manage interactions like calling the doctor without freezing
jtbd.current_approach: Struggles -- couldn't pronounce the doctor's surname on a recent call

jtbd.desired_outcome: Produce Dutch sounds she currently can't, despite understanding the words
jtbd.current_approach: No method is working; says her brain can't work around pronunciation

pains.pain: Production and pronunciation gap -- understands but can't speak
pains.trigger: Real situations like phoning the doctor
pains.cost: Can't function in spoken Dutch; embarrassment; pronunciation feels impossible
pains.verbatim: My vocabulary is okayish, I understand a lot, but speaking and pronounciation is... NNNO. This is something that my brain just cannot work around.

pains.pain: People instantly switch to English when she tries
Course content was mismatched and condescending
pains.trigger: Two lessons at the public library
pains.cost: Quit after two lessons; content aimed at manual-labor scenarios irrelevant to her
pains.verbatim: The sentences they were teaching were like: Hello, my name is Monika, I'm here to clean your room... NOPE.

pains.cost: Wasted effort on phrases she'd never use
pains.verbatim: I learned how to say phrases like: my elephant is green. Where am I gonna use this?

pains.pain: Private tutoring is out of budget

pains.cost: Calls Dutch her 'nightmare'; harder than other languages she learned
Feels her freelance prospects are limited because some clients prefer Dutch

pains.trigger: Moving to a new apartment and getting her driving license
pains.cost: Can't begin a course before around October

workflow_reality.current_process: Informal pickup -- spends time with Dutch people to absorb vocabulary; tried Duolingo, YouTube videos, and two library lessons, all abandoned
workflow_reality.brittleness: Speaking and pronunciation; people switch to English; courses pitched at the wrong level and audience; tutoring unaffordable
workarounds: Spending time with Dutch people to absorb vocabulary informally

alternatives_and_competitors.name: Duolingo
alternatives_and_competitors.context: Hated it -- taught useless phrases like 'my elephant is green'

alternatives_and_competitors.name: YouTube basic-Dutch videos
alternatives_and_competitors.context: Tried as a free supplement; no lasting result

alternatives_and_competitors.name: Public library (bibliotheek) lessons
alternatives_and_competitors.context: Quit after two lessons -- content aimed at cleaners/construction workers, bad atmosphere

alternatives_and_competitors.name: Private tutor
alternatives_and_competitors.context: Considered but ruled out as too expensive for her budget

quotes.text: After 4 years here, my Dutch is still inexistent. I speak 2 languages fluently: English and Polish, and a little Spanish, which was way easier... Dutch is my nightmare, it's soooo difficult to pronounce.
quotes.speaker: prospect -- Polish freelance illustrator
quotes.context: Opening lament about how hard Dutch has been compared to other languages

quotes.text: My vocabulary is okayish, I understand a lot, but speaking and pronounciation is... NNNO. This is something that my brain just cannot work around.
quotes.speaker: prospect -- Polish freelance illustrator

quotes.text: I learned how to say phrases like: my elephant is green. Where am I gonna use this?
quotes.speaker: prospect -- Polish freelance illustrator

quotes.text: I just want to feel like part of the community especially now that we decided to settle. I want to speak Dutch and meet people who are not expats. This is limiting my possibilities.
quotes.speaker: prospect -- Polish freelance illustrator
quotes.context: Her core motivation -- belonging plus winning Dutch clients

value_language: meet people who are not expats
deviant_terms: my brain just cannot work around
